---
title: YAML Settings
---

The YAML front matter at the top of every `.qmd` file configures metadata, output options, and custom variables. *Calepin* parses the block between `---` delimiters before processing the document body.

```yaml
---
title: My Document
author: Jane Doe
date: 2025-01-15
format: html
---
```


# Standard fields

## Document metadata

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `title` | string | Document title |
| `subtitle` | string | Document subtitle |
| `author` | string or list | Author name(s) |
| `date` | string | Publication date |
| `abstract` | string | Abstract text |
| `keywords` | list | Keywords |

## Output

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `format` | string | Output format: `html`, `latex`, `typst`, `markdown` |
| `toc` | boolean | Enable table of contents (default: true for HTML) |
| `toc-depth` | number | Heading depth for TOC (default: 3) |
| `toc-title` | string | TOC title (default: "Contents") |
| `number-sections` | boolean | Number section headings |

## Bibliography

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `bibliography` | string or list | Path(s) to `.bib` file(s) |
| `csl` | string | Path to CSL style file |

## Includes

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `header-includes` | string | Raw content inserted in `<head>` (HTML) or preamble (LaTeX) |
| `include-before` | string | Content inserted before the body |
| `include-after` | string | Content inserted after the body |
| `css` | string or list | Additional CSS files (HTML only) |

## LaTeX options

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `documentclass` | string | LaTeX document class (default: `article`) |
| `classoption` | string | Class options (default: `11pt`) |


# Rich author metadata

Authors can be simple strings or detailed objects:

```yaml
# Simple
author: Jane Doe

# Multiple authors
author:
  - Jane Doe
  - John Smith

# Rich metadata
author:
  - name:
      given: Jane
      family: Doe
    email: jane@example.com
    orcid: 0000-0001-2345-6789
    corresponding: true
    affiliations:
      - ref: uni

affiliations:
  - id: uni
    name: University of Example
    department: Department of Statistics
    city: Exampleville
    country: Examplia
```

Rich author data is rendered through the `author_block`, `author_item`, and `affiliation_item` element templates, which you can override. See [Elements](elements.html).


# Custom fields

Any non-standard YAML field is accessible in document bodies via {% raw %}`{{ var.key }}`{% endraw %}, with dot-notation for nested values. The same fields are also available as page template variables (see [Pages](pages.html)).

```yaml
---
title: Report
version: 2.0
confidential: true
urls:
  docs: https://docs.example.com
  api: https://api.example.com
---
```

In the document body:

```
Version: {{ var.version }}
API docs: {{ var.urls.api }}
```

In a page template (`_calepin/templates/calepin.html`):

```html
{% if confidential %}
<div class="watermark">CONFIDENTIAL</div>
{% endif %}
<footer>Version {{version}}</footer>
```

See [Shortcuts](shortcuts.qmd#var) for details.


# Brand

The `brand:` block configures colors, logos, and typography. It follows the [brand.yml specification](https://posit-dev.github.io/brand-yml/):

```yaml
brand:
  color:
    primary: "#0066cc"
    background: "#ffffff"
    foreground: "#222222"
  logo:
    images:
      main:
        path: logo.svg
        alt: "Company logo"
  typography:
    fonts:
      - family: Inter
        source: google
    base: Inter
```

Brand values are available in templates and document bodies via {% raw %}`{{ brand(type="color", name="primary") }}`{% endraw %}. See [Shortcuts](shortcuts.qmd#brand).


# Appendix metadata

These fields generate appendix sections at the end of the document:

```yaml
license:
  text: "Creative Commons Attribution 4.0"
  url: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
  type: CC BY 4.0     # Shorthand: expanded automatically

citation:
  container-title: "Journal of Examples"
  volume: "42"
  issue: "3"
  page: "100-115"
  doi: "10.1234/example"
  issued: "2025"

copyright:
  holder: "Jane Doe"
  year: "2025"

funding:
  - source: "National Science Foundation"
    award: "Grant #12345"
    recipient: "Jane Doe"
```


# Calepin-specific settings

Settings specific to *Calepin* are nested under the `calepin:` key:

```yaml
calepin:
  plugins:
    - myplugin
    - another-plugin
```


# CLI overrides

Use the `-s` flag to override front matter values from the command line:

```
calepin doc.qmd -s title="New Title" -s toc=false
calepin doc.qmd -s documentclass=report -s classoption=12pt
```

This is useful for batch rendering with different configurations.


# Site configuration

For sites, `_calepin.yaml` (or `_site.yml`) provides project-wide settings. The `website:` block configures navigation, sidebar, and other site-wide options. See [Sites](sites.html) for the full reference.

Document-level front matter takes precedence over site configuration for metadata fields like `title`, `author`, and `date`.
